hero.eyebrow: Revenue analytics for solo SaaS founders
hero.headline: See your real MRR without logging into Stripe
hero.subheadline: One dashboard shows MRR, churn, failed payments, and forecasts in 30 seconds. Connect a read-only key and stop clicking through five Stripe screens every morning.
hero.image_prompt: An abstract editorial illustration in dark mode, magazine cover aesthetic. A geometric composition of a single glowing line graph ascending across a deep navy void, rendered as a taut copper wire suspended between floating matte-black monoliths. Soft volumetric light from the lower left, muted emerald accent on one data point. Subtle grid texture fading into shadow. Sense of depth through overlapping translucent planes. Minimal, precise, architectural. No text, no faces, no UI elements, no screenshots. Flat vector meets 3D isometric, with a hint of risograph grain.

social_proof.label: Built on principles you care about

problem.title: Stripe's dashboard buries the numbers you actually check daily.
scenarios: You open Stripe to check yesterday's MRR. Four clicks later, you're staring at a number that includes trials, or doesn't, and you can't remember which.
A customer annualized their $29/mo plan. Is that $29 MRR or $348? Stripe shows one, your spreadsheet shows another, and neither matches what you told an investor last week.
Three subscriptions failed overnight. You won't know until the dunning emails bounce back in a week, because Stripe doesn't surface failed payments on the default view.

solution.title: One page. The four numbers that matter. Refreshed every 15 minutes.
solution.body: Connect Stripe with a read-only restricted key. That's the entire setup. No webhooks to configure, no data warehouse, no ETL. We poll the Stripe API every 15 minutes and cache your numbers so the dashboard loads instantly. You get normalized MRR (annual subs correctly divided by 12), 7/30/90-day deltas, a list of who churned yesterday with tenure, which subscriptions are in payment failure right now, and a forecast for next month based on active subs. Open the page, get the answer, close the tab, get back to building.

features.title: Normalized MRR that actually reconciles
features.body: Annual subs divided by 12. Trials excluded. Discounts applied. The number you'd calculate by hand if you had two hours every morning. See 7, 30, and 90-day change at a glance, with the delta broken down into new, expansion, contraction, and churn.

features.title: Churn with context, not just a count
features.body: See who churned yesterday: name, email, plan, how long they were a customer, and the cancellation reason if they gave one. Sorted by revenue impact so you know which losses deserve a follow-up email and which were always going to happen.

features.title: Failed payments surfaced immediately
features.body: Every subscription currently in payment failure, ranked by MRR at risk. Catch the $199/mo customer whose card expired before Stripe's dunning sequence writes them off. One click to open the customer in Stripe if you want to email them directly.

features.title: Next-month forecast, no crystal ball
features.body: Projected MRR for next month based on active subscriptions, scheduled renewals, known upcoming cancellations, and your trailing churn rate. No machine learning hand-waving, just arithmetic you can verify. Useful for deciding whether you can afford that contractor this quarter.

features.title: Read-only by design
features.body: Connect with a Stripe restricted key scoped to read-only. We literally cannot charge your customers, issue refunds, or modify subscriptions. The security conversation with yourself ends in 30 seconds. Revoke access from your Stripe dashboard any time.

features.title: No webhooks, no config files
features.body: Every other tool asks you to set up webhook endpoints and hope they don't silently fail. We poll the Stripe API every 15 minutes. Your only setup step is pasting a restricted key. Works whether your Stripe account is a week old or eight years old.

who_this_is_for.title: You're in the right place if…
items: You're a solo or two-person SaaS founder doing $1k–$50k MRR on Stripe and checking revenue daily.
You already use Stripe for billing and want the four numbers that matter on one page, not five.
You've tried Baremetrics or ChartMogul and couldn't justify $100+/mo at your current revenue.
You don't need team accounts, custom cohort analysis, or a sales rep — just the numbers, fast.

pricing.tagline: Simple pricing. 7-day free trial. Cancel anytime.
pricing.guarantee: 7-day free trial, no card required. Cancel in one click.

faq.q: How is this different from Baremetrics or ChartMogul?
faq.a: Same core metrics — MRR, churn, failed payments, forecasts — at roughly a tenth of the price. Baremetrics and ChartMogul are built for venture-backed SaaS companies with finance teams, cohort analysis needs, and five-figure MRR minimums to justify the cost. This is built for solo founders at $1k–$50k MRR who want the four numbers that matter and nothing else. If you need segmentation by UTM source or custom cohort retention charts, buy Baremetrics.

faq.q: What Stripe permissions do you need?
faq.a: A restricted API key with read-only scopes on subscriptions, customers, charges, and invoices. That's it. We cannot create charges, issue refunds, modify subscriptions, or access your payout bank info. You generate the key in your Stripe dashboard and paste it during setup — takes about 60 seconds. Revoke it any time from Stripe and we immediately lose all access to your data.

faq.q: Do I need to set up webhooks?
faq.a: No. We poll the Stripe API every 15 minutes and cache your data. Webhooks are the single most common onboarding friction point in SaaS tools because they require customer config, can silently fail, and often need ngrok-style forwarding during testing. Skipping them is a deliberate product decision. Your dashboard lags real-time Stripe by at most 15 minutes, which is fine for a number you check once a day.

Under two minutes. Sign up with email, generate a restricted Stripe key (Stripe has a one-click template), paste it in. We backfill 90 days of history in the background — usually done in 30 to 90 seconds depending on your transaction volume. You'll see MRR, churn, and failed payments on the dashboard before you finish your coffee.

faq.q: Can I cancel and get a refund?
faq.a: Yes. Start with a 7-day free trial — no card required. If you pay and decide within 30 days that it's not useful, email us and we'll refund in full, no questions. After that, cancel any time from your account page and you won't be charged again. No annual lock-in, no cancellation forms, no retention call.

faq.q: Does this support Paddle, Chargebee, or other billing providers?
faq.a: No. Stripe only, by design. Supporting multiple billing backends means normalizing wildly different data models, and in v1 we'd rather nail one than half-support three. If Stripe isn't your billing provider, this isn't the right tool. If you're on Stripe plus something else, we'll show you your Stripe-side numbers accurately — just know they won't include the other provider's revenue.

We store your Stripe metrics in a Supabase Postgres database encrypted at rest. We never store full card numbers or bank info (Stripe never exposes those over the API anyway). We don't sell data, don't run analytics on your metrics, and don't share with third parties. Delete your account from settings and we purge everything within 30 days. Full privacy policy linked in the footer.

faq.q: Do you support multiple Stripe accounts?
faq.a: Yes, on the Pro plan. Connect as many Stripe accounts as you have — useful if you run multiple SaaS products, have a separate test account you actually use for a small side project, or consolidated a co-founder's account. The Pro dashboard shows each account individually and a combined view across all of them. Starter is limited to one Stripe account.

cta_final.headline: Stop clicking through Stripe. Start seeing your numbers.
cta_final.body: 7-day free trial, no credit card required. Takes under two minutes to connect.
cta_final.urgency: Early-bird pricing: $9 and $29 locked in for life for the first 100 customers.
